# codexctl

codexctl is a local-brain companion and terminal dashboard for Codex sessions.

Primary capabilities:
- Discover local Codex rollouts under ~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl.
- Show status, health, cost, context pressure, and supported terminal actions.
- Evaluate pending actions with deterministic rules and a local LLM.
- Keep advisory mode by default; --auto-run executes high-confidence actions.
- Immediate actions: approve, deny, send, terminate, route, and spawn.
- Learn from local decisions, outcomes, preferences, and canonical review examples.
- Deliver best-effort local mailbox messages to live sessions.

Privacy:
- Brain data remains under ~/.codexctl/brain.
- Non-loopback endpoints trigger a warning because transcript context may leave the machine.

Compatibility:
- Config paths remain .codexctl.toml and ~/.config/codexctl/config.toml.
- Legacy relay, hive, idle, external-agent, and retention settings are ignored with warnings.
- Normal upgrade preserves legacy state; codexctl init --purge is destructive.

Durable coordination:
- codexctl has no durable task queue or distributed coordinator.
- Beads or another external tracker can manage tasks, dependencies, claims, blockers, gates, and handoffs.

Docs:
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/quickstart/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/configuration/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/reference/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/terminal-support/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/troubleshooting/
- https://aleadag.github.io/codexctl/contributing/
